_note: TEST ALPHA-3 - CLEAN PAINTED PARRY (completes the miss/block/parry trifecta via OUTPUT). Same architecture as the clean-miss test, but paints a WEAPON PARRY instead of a miss: selector forces evade-type +0x1C0=0x02 (weapon parry) and the pre-clamp forces 0 damage on Ramza. Proves we choose HOW the hit is avoided (miss vs parry vs block) purely in memory, with 0 damage. Floor: force-hit (evade 0 broadcast) + Brave 10 (Blade Grasp suppressed) so every attack is a natural hit to repaint. RUN: relaunch via the Reloaded GUI Launch button (CLI --launch does NOT work here), enter battle with Ramza, attack him from the front, physical melee, 3-4 times. OBSERVE PER ATTACK: (a) does Ramza PARRY (weapon-parry animation, distinct from the plain MISS we saw before and from the Blade-Grasp hand-raise)? (b) does his HP stay the SAME (0 damage)? PASS = parry animation + 0 HP loss on a guaranteed hit = OUTPUT fully controls the avoidance TYPE + damage; miss/block/parry are all paintable. To see BLOCK instead, change ForceEvadeType to 3; for plain MISS, 6. Evade-type enum (live): 0x00 hit, 0x01 cloak, 0x02 weapon parry, 0x03 shield block, 0x04 class evade, 0x06 plain miss. Safe: only Ramza painted + zeroed; struct writes only.
